title: Verify Signal Request Schema
description: Request schema for the verify_signal task in OpenSignals Protocol. Used to validate signal compliance against policies, regulations, and usage restrictions before signal activation or usage.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this verification request (UUID v4 format).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the verification request was initiated.

signal_id.description: Unique identifier of the signal to be verified. Must match a registered signal_id in the OpenSignals ecosystem.

signal_version.description: Specific version of the signal manifest to verify against. If omitted, latest version is used.

organization_id.description: Unique identifier of the organization requesting verification.

organization_name.description: Legal name of the requesting organization.

user_id.description: Unique identifier of the user initiating the verification request.

email.description: Email address of the requesting user.

role.description: Role of the requesting user within their organization.

requestor.description: Information about the entity requesting signal verification.

verification_type.description: Type of verification being requested. 'pre_activation' = before signal goes live, 'periodic_review' = scheduled compliance check, 'incident_investigation' = in response to an issue, 'policy_change' = after policy updates, 'usage_validation' = verify specific usage scenario, 'compliance_audit' = regulatory audit, 'manual_review' = ad-hoc human review.

intended_use.description: Intended use cases for the signal being verified.

geographic_scope.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes where signal will be used.

target_audience.description: Target audience characteristics for signal usage.

advertiser_id.description: Identifier of the advertiser using this signal.

product_categories.description: Product or service categories being advertised.

platforms.description: Advertising platforms where signal will be used.

data_retention_days.description: Number of days the requestor intends to retain signal data.

context.description: Contextual information about how the signal will be used.

policy_ids.description: Specific policy IDs to verify against. If empty, all applicable policies are checked.

policy_frameworks.description: Policy frameworks to verify compliance against.

rule_id.description: Unique identifier for the custom rule.

description.description: Description of the custom verification rule.

severity.description: Severity level of the rule violation.

custom_rules.description: Custom verification rules specific to the requestor.

policies.description: Policy and compliance requirements for verification.

consent_available.description: Whether user consent is available for the intended signal usage.

consent_string.description: Encoded consent string (e.g., IAB TCF consent string).

purposes_consented.description: List of purpose IDs for which consent was granted.

consent_status.description: User consent information relevant to signal usage.

strict_mode.description: If true, apply strictest interpretation of policies and fail on any ambiguity.

include_warnings.description: Whether to include non-blocking warnings in verification response.

require_manual_review.description: Whether to require manual human review regardless of automated checks.

generate_recommendations.description: Whether to generate recommendations for addressing verification issues.

detailed_report.description: Whether to generate a detailed verification report with all checks.

verification_options.description: Options controlling verification behavior and output.

callback_url.description: Optional webhook URL to receive verification results asynchronously.

priority.description: Priority level for processing this verification request.

metadata.description: Additional metadata for the verification request.

notes.description: Additional notes or context from the requestor.

tags.description: Freeform tags for categorization and search.
